label: 103_usmca_north american_north america_usmca forward
description: Analysis of the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement's impact on North American supply chains, focusing on integration and security. Experts from all three countries contribute to reports published in 2023 and 2024.

earliest_articles.title: Five Flaws in the USMCA and How to Fix Them
earliest_articles.excerpt: : It does not address global warming (also shunted aside by Obama negotiators for fear of losing Republican support for the TPP). The growing crisis is too critical to ignore any longer. At the very least, the USMCA should promote investment and trade in renewable energy resources and other measures to encourage…

earliest_articles.title: The importance of USMCA for the Biden administration’s economic and foreign policy
earliest_articles.excerpt: In her confirmation hearing, U.S. Trade Representative Katherine Tai stated that “trade is like any other tool in our domestic or foreign policy. It is a means to create more hope and opportunity.” With this in mind, President Biden has made clear that trade must improve wages and create higher-paying jobs for all…

earliest_articles.title: Developing a roadmap for USMCA success
earliest_articles.excerpt: Introduction The passage of the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) through the U.S. Congress with overwhelming bipartisan support, as well as with strong political backing in Canada and Mexico, underscored the importance of USMCA for North American trade and economic relations.1 It builds on the North…

newest_articles.title: USMCA review reviewed: Lessons from the first use of USMCA’s review mechanism
newest_articles.excerpt: The first review of the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) has now concluded. Canada and Mexico agreed to extend the agreement for another 16 years, while the United States declined to do so. As a result, USMCA remains in force while Article 34.7 initiates a process of annual joint reviews, creating…

newest_articles.title: United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement 2026: Review and déjà vu
newest_articles.excerpt: This foreword is part of USMCA Forward 2026. Eight years ago, in the second year of U.S. President Donald Trump’s first term in office, North America was in a state of flux. The terms of the North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA) that had governed regional trade for a quarter century were being renegotiated.…

central_articles.title: Strengthening USMCA and North America’s economic cooperation
central_articles.excerpt: This chapter is part of USMCA Forward 2024. Introduction The United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) will reach its fourth anniversary in 2024 just as Mexico elects a new president-a few months before the United States does the same.
